sourceQuestion:
Using longer identifiers in a program will necessarily lead to:

A. Somewhat slower compilation
B. A program that is easier to understand
C. An incorrect program
D. None of the above

parsed.stem: Using longer identifiers in a program will necessarily lead to:

options.text: A program that is easier to understand

B. 26
C. 10

sourceQuestion:
Which data structure in a compiler is used for managing information about variables and their attributes?

A. Abstract syntax tree
B. Symbol table
C. Semantic stack
D. Parse table

sourceQuestion:
In a compiler, keywords of a language are recognized during
A. parsing of the program
B. the code generation
C. the lexical analysis of the program
D. dataflow analysis
sourceAnswer: C. the lexical analysis of the program

parsed.stem: In a compiler, keywords of a language are recognized during

options.text: the lexical analysis of the program

sourceQuestion:
The lexical analysis for a modern computer language such as Java needs the power of which one of the following machine models in a necessary and sufficient sense?
A. Finite state automata
B. Deterministic pushdown automata
C. Non-deterministic pushdown automata
D. Turing machine

parsed.stem: The lexical analysis for a modern computer language such as Java needs the power of which one of the following machine models in a necessary and sufficient sense?

sourceQuestion:
A lexical analyzer uses the following patterns to recognize three tokens T_1, T_2, and T_3 over the alphabet {a, b, c}:
T_1: a?(b|c)*a
T_2: b?(a|c)*b
T_3: c?(a|b)*c
Note that '?' means zero or one occurrence of the symbol. Note also that the analyzer outputs the token that matches the longest possible prefix.
If the string 'aab' is processed by the analyzer, which one of the following is the sequence of tokens output?
A. T_1, T_2
B. T_1, T_1
C. T_2, T_1
D. T_3, T_1

parsed.stem:
A lexical analyzer uses the following patterns to recognize three tokens T_1, T_2, and T_3 over the alphabet {a, b, c}:
T_1: a?(b|c)*a
T_2: b?(a|c)*b
T_3: c?(a|b)*c
Note that '?' means zero or one occurrence of the symbol. Note also that the analyzer outputs the token that matches the longest possible prefix.
If the string 'aab' is processed by the analyzer, which one of the following is the sequence of tokens output?

sourceQuestion:
A 'link editor' is a program that (select all that apply):
A. matches the parameters of the macro-definition with locations of the parameters of the macro call
B. matches external names of one program with their location in other programs
C. matches the parameters of subroutine definition with the location of parameters of subroutine call
D. acts as a link between text editor and the user
E. acts as a link between compiler and loader

matches external names of one program with their location in other programs
matches the parameters of subroutine definition with the location of parameters of subroutine call
acts as a link between text editor and the user
acts as a link between compiler and loader

sourceQuestion:
Which of the following is NOT an advantage of using shared, dynamically linked libraries as opposed to using statically linked libraries?
A. Smaller sizes of executable files
B. Lesser overall page fault rate in the system
C. Faster program startup
D. Existing programs need not be re-linked to take advantage of newer versions of libraries

parsed.stem: Which of the following is NOT an advantage of using shared, dynamically linked libraries as opposed to using statically linked libraries?

Existing programs need not be re-linked to take advantage of newer versions of libraries

A. Edit time
B. Compile time
C. Link time
D. Load time

parsed.stem: Consider a program P that consists of two source modules M_1 and M_2 contained in two different files. If M_1 contains a reference to a function defined in M_2, the reference will be resolved at

sourceQuestion:
A variable x is said to be live at a statement S_i in a program if the following three conditions hold simultaneously:
i.   There exists a statement S_j that uses x.
ii.  There is a path from S_i to S_j in the flow graph corresponding to the program.
iii. The path has no intervening assignment to x including at S_i and not at S_j.

The variables that are live both at the statement in basic block 2 and the statement in basic block 3 of the following control flow graph are:

[Control flow graph with 4 basic blocks:
Block 1: p = 1
Block 2: q = p + 1; s = q
Block 3: p = 3; r = p
Block 4: u = r + s
Edges: Block 1 -> Block 2, Block 1 -> Block 3, Block 2 -> Block 4, Block 3 -> Block 4]

A. p, q, u
B. r, s, u
C. r, u
D. q, v

parsed.stem:
A variable x is said to be live at a statement S_i in a program if the following three conditions hold simultaneously:
i.   There exists a statement S_j that uses x.
ii.  There is a path from S_i to S_j in the flow graph corresponding to the program.
iii. The path has no intervening assignment to x including at S_i and not at S_j.

The variables that are live both at the statement in basic block 2 and the statement in basic block 3 of the following control flow graph are:

[Control flow graph with 4 basic blocks:
Block 1: p = 1
Block 2: q = p + 1; s = q
Block 3: p = 3; r = p
Block 4: u = r + s
Edges: Block 1 -> Block 2, Block 1 -> Block 3, Block 2 -> Block 4, Block 3 -> Block 4]

sourceQuestion:
For a statement S in a program, in the context of liveness analysis, the following sets are defined:
USE(S): the set of variables used in S
IN(S): the set of variables that are live at the entry of S
OUT(S): the set of variables that are live at the exit of S

Consider a basic block B that consists of two statements, S_1 followed by S_2. Which one of the following statements is correct?

A. OUT(B) = IN(S_2)
B. OUT(B) = IN(S_2) ∪ USE(S_2)
C. OUT(B) = IN(S_2) ∪ OUT(S_1)
D. OUT(B) = USE(S_1) ∪ IN(S_2)

parsed.stem:
For a statement S in a program, in the context of liveness analysis, the following sets are defined:
USE(S): the set of variables used in S
IN(S): the set of variables that are live at the entry of S
OUT(S): the set of variables that are live at the exit of S

Consider a basic block B that consists of two statements, S_1 followed by S_2. Which one of the following statements is correct?

sourceQuestion:
Consider the control flow graph shown below:

B1:
  i = 1
  j = 1
B2:
  if (i <= 10) goto B3 else goto B4
B3:
  a = a + j
  i = i + 1
  goto B2
B4:
  return a

(B1 -> B2 -> B3 -> B2 (loop), B2 -> B4 on exit)

Which one of the following choices correctly lists the set of live variables at the exit point of each basic block?

A. B1: { }, B2: {a}, B3: {a}, B4: {a}
B. B1: {i, j}, B2: {a}, B3: {a}, B4: {a}
C. B1: {a, i, j}, B2: {a, i, j}, B3: {a, i, j}, B4: {a}
D. B1: {i, j}, B2: {a, i, j}, B3: {a, i, j}, B4: { }

parsed.stem:
Consider the control flow graph shown below:

B1:
  i = 1
  j = 1
B2:
  if (i <= 10) goto B3 else goto B4
B3:
  a = a + j
  i = i + 1
  goto B2
B4:
  return a

(B1 -> B2 -> B3 -> B2 (loop), B2 -> B4 on exit)

Which one of the following choices correctly lists the set of live variables at the exit point of each basic block?

sourceQuestion:
What are x and y in the following macro definition?

macro Add x, y
  Load y
  Mul x
  Store y
end macro

A. Variables
B. Identifiers
C. Actual parameters
D. Formal parameters

parsed.stem:

sourceQuestion:
Which of the following macros can put a macro assembler into an infinite loop?

i.  .MACRO M1, X
    .IF EQ, X       ;if X = 0 then
      M1 X+1
    .ENDC
    .IF NE, X       ;if X != 0 then
      .WORD X       ;address (X) is stored here
    .ENDC
    .ENDM

ii. .MACRO M2, X
    .IF EQ, X       ;if X = 0 then
      M2 X
    .ENDC
    .IF NE, X       ;if X != 0 then
      .WORD X
    .ENDC
    .ENDM

A. (i) only
B. (ii) only
C. Both (i) and (ii)
D. None of the above

parsed.stem:
Which of the following macros can put a macro assembler into an infinite loop?

i.  .MACRO M1, X
    .IF EQ, X       ;if X = 0 then
      M1 X+1
    .ENDC
    .IF NE, X       ;if X != 0 then
      .WORD X       ;address (X) is stored here
    .ENDC
    .ENDM

ii. .MACRO M2, X
    .IF EQ, X       ;if X = 0 then
      M2 X
    .ENDC
    .IF NE, X       ;if X != 0 then
      .WORD X
    .ENDC
    .ENDM

sourceQuestion:
The conditional expansion facility of macro processor is provided to:
A. test a condition during the execution of the expanded program
B. to expand certain model statements depending upon the value of a condition during the execution of the expanded program
C. to implement recursion
D. to expand certain model statements depending upon the value of a condition during the process of macro expansion
sourceAnswer: D. to expand certain model statements depending upon the value of a condition during the process of macro expansion

parsed.stem: The conditional expansion facility of macro processor is provided to:

options.text: test a condition during the execution of the expanded program
to expand certain model statements depending upon the value of a condition during the execution of the expanded program
to expand certain model statements depending upon the value of a condition during the process of macro expansion

sourceQuestion:
Given the following expression grammar:
E -> E * F | F + E | F
F -> F - F | id

Which of the following is true?
A. * has higher precedence than +
B. - has higher precedence than *
C. + and - have same precedence
D. + has higher precedence than *

parsed.stem:
Given the following expression grammar:
E -> E * F | F + E | F
F -> F - F | id

Which of the following is true?

sourceQuestion:
Consider the operator precedence and associativity rules for the integer arithmetic operators given in the table below.

| Operator | Precedence | Associativity |
|----------|------------|---------------|
|    /     |  Highest   |     Right     |
|    *     |    High    |     Right     |
|    +     |   Medium   |     Left      |
|    -     |    Low     |     Left      |

The value of the expression 3 + 1 + 5 * 2 / 1 - 2 * 4 - 7 + 3 as per the above rules is ________.
A. 2
B. 6
C. 30
D. -15

parsed.stem:
Consider the operator precedence and associativity rules for the integer arithmetic operators given in the table below.

| Operator | Precedence | Associativity |
|----------|------------|---------------|
|    /     |  Highest   |     Right     |
|    *     |    High    |     Right     |
|    +     |   Medium   |     Left      |
|    -     |    Low     |     Left      |

The value of the expression 3 + 1 + 5 * 2 / 1 - 2 * 4 - 7 + 3 as per the above rules is ________.

sourceAnswer: B. No, it is incorrect because the index i might change inside P, so A[i] at the end might be a different element than A[i] at the start.

options.text: No, it is incorrect because the index i might change inside P, so A[i] at the end might be a different element than A[i] at the start.
No, it is incorrect because B[i] may not equal i after execution of P.
Yes, it is correct only when i does not change inside P.

sourceQuestion:
In which of the following case(s) is it possible to obtain different results for call-by-reference and call-by-name parameter passing?

A. Passing an expression as a parameter
B. Passing an array as a parameter
C. Passing a pointer as a parameter
D. Passing an array element as a parameter

Passing an array as a parameter
Passing a pointer as a parameter
Passing an array element as a parameter

sourceQuestion:
Indicate all the true statements from the following:

A. Recursive descent parsing cannot be used for grammar with left recursion.
B. The intermediate form for representing expressions which is best suited for code optimization is the postfix form.
C. A programming language not supporting either recursion or pointer type does not need the support of dynamic memory allocation.
D. Although C does not support call-by-name parameter passing, the effect can be correctly simulated in C.
E. No feature of Pascal typing violates strong typing in Pascal.

parsed.stem: Indicate all the true statements from the following:

options.text: Recursive descent parsing cannot be used for grammar with left recursion.
The intermediate form for representing expressions which is best suited for code optimization is the postfix form.
A programming language not supporting either recursion or pointer type does not need the support of dynamic memory allocation.
Although C does not support call-by-name parameter passing, the effect can be correctly simulated in C.
No feature of Pascal typing violates strong typing in Pascal.

sourceAnswer: A. Static: a=5, b=10; Dynamic: a=1, b=10

options.text: Static: a=5, b=10; Dynamic: a=1, b=10
Static: a=5, b=10; Dynamic: a=5, b=10
Static: a=1, b=10; Dynamic: a=1, b=10
Static: a=5, b=10; Dynamic: a=2, b=10

sourceAnswer: C. call swap(&x, &y) cannot be used as the parameters are passed by value

options.text: call swap(x, y) cannot be used as it does not return any value
call swap(x, y) cannot be used as it does not return any value
call swap(&x, &y) cannot be used as the parameters are passed by value
swap(x, y) must be called followed by x = a; y = b;

sourceAnswer: C. Call by value: i = 100, j = 60; Call by reference: i = 10, j = 80

options.text: Call by value: i = 70, j = 60; Call by reference: i = 60, j = 70
Call by value: i = 100, j = 60; Call by reference: i = 100, j = 170
Call by value: i = 100, j = 60; Call by reference: i = 10, j = 80
Call by value: i = 100, j = 60; Call by reference: i = 100, j = 60

sourceQuestion:
A context-free grammar is ambiguous if:

A. The grammar contains useless non-terminals.
B. It produces more than one parse tree for some sentence.
C. Some production has two non-terminals side by side on the right-hand side.
D. None of the above.
sourceAnswer: B. It produces more than one parse tree for some sentence.

parsed.stem: A context-free grammar is ambiguous if:

options.text: It produces more than one parse tree for some sentence.
Some production has two non-terminals side by side on the right-hand side.
